title: Enable Google account connection for automatic YouTube channel posting

requirements.text: Implement Google OAuth 2.0 authentication flow in the backend to allow users to connect their Google accounts and authorize the app to post videos to their YouTube channels

quotes: Can you make it so they can connect their google account so the app auto posts it to their channel

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores Google OAuth tokens securely for each user
Users can authorize YouTube channel access through Google OAuth
Backend can refresh expired OAuth tokens automatically

requirements.text: Modify the existing ChannelConnection component to use Google OAuth instead of the current mock implementation, displaying the connected Google account and YouTube channel information

quotes: Can you make it so they can connect their google account so the app auto posts it to their channel

acceptanceCriteria: Connect button initiates real Google OAuth flow
Connected state shows the user's Google account email and YouTube channel name
Disconnect button revokes access and clears stored tokens

requirements.text: Update the existing postToYouTube backend method to use the stored Google OAuth credentials to automatically post clips to the user's connected YouTube channel without requiring manual interaction

quotes: so the app auto posts it to their channel

acceptanceCriteria: Clips are posted directly to the user's YouTube channel using their OAuth credentials
Posting happens automatically when the user clicks the post button
Error handling for expired or invalid OAuth tokens with user feedback

requirements.text: Add backend validation to ensure users have connected their Google account before allowing them to post clips to YouTube
acceptanceCriteria: postToYouTube method returns an error if no Google account is connected
Backend checks for valid OAuth tokens before attempting to post

requirements.text: Update the ClipCard component to disable the YouTube post button and show a helpful message when the user has not connected their Google account
acceptanceCriteria: Post to YouTube button is disabled when no Google account is connected
Tooltip or message directs users to connect their Google account first
Button becomes enabled once Google account is successfully connected

constraints: Must use Google OAuth 2.0 for authentication (not custom authentication)
OAuth tokens must be stored securely per user in the backend
Must work within Internet Computer's single-actor architecture
Cannot use external databases or third-party backend services
nonGoals: Building a full video editing interface like creator.reka.ai
Advanced timeline editing or multi-track video features
Automatic clip generation without user interaction
Posting to social media platforms other than YouTube
